question_title: Will at least one film surpass US$1 billion in worldwide box office before May 1, 2026?
summary: The question hinges almost entirely on one film: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie (April 1, 2026). Avatar: Fire and Ash ($1.47B total) is confirmed NOT listed on BOM's 2026 worldwide page - it's classified as a 2025 release. No other film before May 1 has $1B potential. The original Super Mario Bros Movie took ~26 days from first screenings to cross $1B ($1.022B), and the sequel gets exactly 30 days. With ~10-12% ticket price inflation since 2023, the sequel needs roughly 87-89% of the original's attendance pace. Historical data shows 6/7 major animated franchise sequels achieved this threshold. Monte Carlo simulations across conservative assumptions center around 50-52% probability. Adding a small ~5% chance Avatar gets counted via alternative resolution interpretation brings the total to ~54%.

factors.description: Avatar: Fire and Ash ($1.47B total) is confirmed NOT on BOM's 2026 worldwide page - classified as 2025 release. The most obvious path to YES is eliminated.
Super Mario Galaxy Movie releases April 1, 2026 - only 30 days before deadline. Original crossed $1B at ~day 26 from first screenings, so timing is very tight.
Sequel has 4-day timing cushion vs when the original crossed $1B. Original hit $1.02B at day 26; sequel has until day 30.
Ticket price inflation ~10-12% over 3 years means sequel needs only ~87-89% of original's attendance to match gross. Meaningful mechanical buffer.
Historical animated sequel base rate: 6/7 major franchise sequels (Zootopia 2, Frozen 2, Incredibles 2, Finding Dory, Toy Story 4, DM3) achieved >=87% of original's gross.
Original Mario movie had unique novelty factor (first quality video game movie in decades). Sequel can't replicate this one-time cultural event aspect.
No other film releasing before May 1, 2026 has any realistic chance at $1B worldwide. All eggs are in the Mario basket.
Small probability (~5%) that resolution creator counts Avatar despite BOM classification, since Avatar objectively earned >$1B in 2026 calendar year.
Mario is among world's most recognized IPs. Galaxy is beloved game. Zootopia 2 (2025) proved animated sequels can massively outperform originals.

reasoning:
## Summary

**Probability: 0.54**

The question resolves based on BOM's 2026 worldwide page. The critical finding is that **Avatar: Fire and Ash is NOT listed on that page** despite earning $1.47B total — BOM categorizes it as a 2025 release.

The only realistic path to YES is **The Super Mario Galaxy Movie** (April 1, 2026), which would need to reach $1B in just 30 days. The original Mario movie took ~26 days from first screenings to cross $1B, so this is feasible but tight. With ~10-12% ticket price inflation since 2023, the sequel only needs ~87-89% of the original's audience. Historical animated sequel data shows 6/7 major franchises achieved this threshold.

Monte Carlo simulations across conservative assumptions center around 50-52% probability for the Mario movie alone, with a small additional probability from Avatar interpretation risk, yielding **~54% overall**.
